key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $2.14 vs $4.54 in Q1 2025
R&D expense of $3.53 billion (14.7% of sales)
qoq_changes: Cash and cash equivalents increased from $19.71 billion to $21.69 billion
Short-term loans and notes payable surged from $8.50 billion to $17.46 billion
Long-term debt decreased slightly from $39.44 billion to $37.53 billion
new_risks: Risks related to the planned separation of the Orthopaedics business, including failure to consummate or realize anticipated benefits
Impact of inflation, interest rate fluctuations, and global tensions on supply chain and margins
management_tone: Cautiously optimistic; while top-line sales grew nearly 10%, management heavily emphasizes execution risks surrounding the planned Orthopaedics business separation and ongoing macroeconomic pressures.
analyst_note: JNJ delivered solid top-line growth of 9.9% YoY, though net income fell sharply by 52% due to a tough comparison against a $7.3B one-time 'other income' gain in the prior year. The upcoming separation of the Orthopaedics business and a significant increase in short-term debt warrant close monitoring.
